report_name: CloudZ RAT Exploits Windows Phone Link to Intercept SMS and OTPs from Phones

attack_patterns.name: User Execution: Malicious File
attack_patterns.description: The initial infection occurs when a victim is tricked into running a malicious file disguised as a legitimate update for ConnectWise ScreenConnect.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Local System
attack_patterns.description: The Pheno plugin accesses and reads the local SQLite database (`PhoneExperiences-*.db`) created by the Microsoft Phone Link application to steal synchronized SMS messages and notifications.

attack_patterns.name: Virtualization/Sandbox Evasion
attack_patterns.description: The CloudZ RAT performs checks for debuggers and virtualized environments to avoid analysis.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: Stolen data, including SMS messages and OTPs, is exfiltrated to an attacker-controlled C2 server by the CloudZ RAT.

attack_patterns.name: Multi-Factor Authentication Interception
attack_patterns.description: The attack intercepts SMS and notification-based OTPs by reading them from the Phone Link database on the compromised PC, allowing attackers to bypass 2FA.

malware.name: CloudZ RAT
malware.description: A remote access tool deployed via a fake ConnectWise ScreenConnect update. It features anti-analysis capabilities and uses the Pheno plugin to steal SMS messages and OTPs from the victim's PC.

malware.name: Pheno
malware.description: A custom plugin for the CloudZ RAT specifically designed to access and read the local Microsoft Phone Link SQLite database to steal SMS messages and OTPs.

tools.name: Microsoft Phone Link
tools.description: Legitimate Microsoft application abused by the Pheno plugin to access synchronized mobile data stored in a local SQLite database on the PC.

tools.name: ConnectWise ScreenConnect
tools.description: The name of this legitimate remote access software was used as a disguise for the malicious dropper file to trick users into execution.

identities.name: Microsoft
identities.description: Vendor of the abused Phone Link application and the Windows operating system.

identities.name: ConnectWise
identities.description: Vendor of the ScreenConnect software whose name was used as a lure for the initial access dropper.

identities.name: Cisco Talos
identities.description: The security research organization that identified and reported on the CloudZ RAT campaign.

infrastructure.name: CloudZ C2 Server
infrastructure.description: Command-and-control server used by the CloudZ RAT to exfiltrate stolen data, such as SMS messages and OTPs.

indicators.name: Fake ConnectWise ScreenConnect Client
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Fake ConnectWise ScreenConnect Client Execution
id: f1a2b3c4-d5e6-4789-a0b1-c2d3e4f5a6b7
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of a process masquerading as the ConnectWise ScreenConnect client, which is used as a dropper for CloudZ RAT.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/05/windows-phone-link-exploited-by-cloudz.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-06
tags:
  - attack.t1204.002
  - attack.t1036.005
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\ConnectWise.ScreenConnect.Client.exe'
  filter_legit_path:
    Image|startswith:
      - 'C:\Program Files (x86)\ScreenConnect'
      - 'C:\Program Files\ScreenConnect'
  condition: selection and not filter_legit_path
indicators.description: Detects the execution of a process masquerading as the ConnectWise ScreenConnect client, used as a dropper for CloudZ RAT.

indicators.name: Anomalous Access to Microsoft Phone Link Database
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Access to Microsoft Phone Link Database
id: e8d7c6b5-a493-4210-bf98-a7b6c5d4e3f2
status: experimental
description: Detects processes other than the legitimate PhoneExperienceHost.exe accessing the Microsoft Phone Link SQLite database. This is indicative of the CloudZ RAT's Pheno plugin stealing SMS and OTP data.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/05/windows-phone-link-exploited-by-cloudz.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-06
tags:
  - attack.t1005
  - attack.t1111
level: high
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|contains|all:
      - '\AppData\Local\Packages\Microsoft.YourPhone_'
      - '\LocalState\PhoneExperiences-'
    TargetFilename|endswith: '.db'
  filter:
    Image|endswith: '\PhoneExperienceHost.exe'
  condition: selection and not filter
indicators.description: Detects non-standard processes accessing the Microsoft Phone Link database, a behavior associated with the Pheno plugin.

relationships.source_name: Anomalous Access to Microsoft Phone Link Database
